questions.question: The pH paper shows a pH of ______ for Solution A.
questions.explanation: Raghu conducts a pH paper test on three different solutions, and the pH paper shows a pH of 9 for Solution A.

questions.question: A compound formed by combining Aluminum ion (Al³⁺) and ______ is AlCl₃.
questions.explanation: If you combine the aluminum ion (Al³⁺) and chloride ions using the crisscross method, the resulting formula of the compound formed is AlCl₃.

questions.question: The characteristic or use of Non-metal Bromine (Br) is as a ______ liquid non-metal.
questions.explanation: Match the non-metal with its characteristic or use: A. Chlorine (Cl), B. Bromine (Br), C. Iodine (I), D. Phosphorus (P).

questions.question: The alloy Stainless steel is composed of ______% copper, ______% zinc.
questions.explanation: Match the alloy with its components: Alloy Component A. Stainless steel, B. Brass, C. Bronze.

questions.question: The equipment used to measure the volume of liquids is a ______ container.
questions.explanation: A tall, cylindrical container used to measure the volume of liquids. A heat source used to heat substances.

questions.question: The compound Sodium Chloride is composed of ______ and Cl⁻ ions.
questions.explanation: Match the following. Compound Ions and Charges A. Sodium Chloride, B. Calcium Oxide, C. Magnesium Chloride.

questions.question: The equipment used to transfer small volumes of liquids is a ______.
questions.explanation: Equipment Description A. Bunsen Burner, B. Funnel, C. Pipette.

questions.question: The equipment used to heat substances is a ______.
questions.explanation: Equipment Description A. Bunsen Burner, B. Funnel, C. Pipette.

questions.question: The equipment used to holds liquids for heating is a ______.
questions.explanation: Equipment Description A. Bunsen Burner, B. Funnel, C. Pipette.

questions.question: The pH paper shows a pH of ______ for Solution B.
questions.explanation: Raghu conducts a pH paper test on three different solutions, and the pH paper shows a pH of 3 for Solution B.

questions.question: The compound Magnesium Chloride is composed of Mg²⁺ and ______ ions.
questions.explanation: Match the following. Compound Ions and Charges A. Sodium Chloride, B. Calcium Oxide, C. Magnesium Chloride.

questions.question: The equipment used to measure the temperature of a liquid during a reaction is a ______.
questions.explanation: Task 3: To measure the temperature of a liquid during a reaction. Which of the following sets of tools would you use for the above tasks?

questions.question: The compound Calcium Oxide is composed of Ca²⁺ and ______ ions.
questions.explanation: Match the following. Compound Ions and Charges A. Sodium Chloride, B. Calcium Oxide, C. Magnesium Chloride.

questions.question: The equipment used to transfer small volumes of liquids is a ______ for Task 1.
questions.explanation: Task 1: To accurately measure and transfer 25 mL of a liquid into a beaker. Which of the following sets of tools would you use for the above tasks?

questions.question: The equipment used to heat substances is a ______ for Task 2.
questions.explanation: Task 2: To heat a solid substance to a high temperature for a reaction. Which of the following sets of tools would you use for the above tasks?
